summary: Russia launched a large-scale overnight attack on Ukraine with an Iskander-M ballistic missile and 90 drones, targeting multiple regions including gas stations and energy infrastructure. Ukrainian forces struck the Poltava oil base in Krasnodar Krai for the second time in a month, hitting at least three fuel tanks, while also downing an Iskander-M that struck Kyiv's Darnytskyi district. The EU disbursed €3.2 billion to Ukraine as the first tranche of a €90 billion loan, while US Secretary of State Rubio confirmed no agreement was reached between Putin and Trump in Anchorage.
key_points: Russia attacked Ukraine overnight with an Iskander-M ballistic missile and 90 drones, targeting gas stations in Zaporizhzhia and Sumy regions, injuring civilians including two gas station workers and one woman. (Source: @UkraineNow/82162)
Ukraine struck the Poltava oil base in Krasnodar Krai for the second time in a month, hitting at least three fuel tanks supplying fuel to Kuban and the Republic of Adygea. (Source: @UkraineNow/82160)
An Iskander-M ballistic missile struck Kyiv's Darnytskyi district, causing a fire at a warehouse facility and debris falling on open territory. (Source: @UkraineNow/82181)
The EU disbursed €3.2 billion to Ukraine as the first macro-financial aid tranche within a €90 billion loan framework, and announced funding for drone production. (Source: @UkraineNow/82171)
US Secretary of State Rubio stated that only a proposal was made at the Putin-Trump meeting in Anchorage but no final agreement was reached on Ukraine settlement. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31160)
France detained the sanctioned tanker Deliver belonging to Russia's shadow fleet, which was transiting near Sicily in the Mediterranean Sea in violation of maritime law. (Source: @UkraineNow/82172)

Ukraine's Defense Forces raised the blue-yellow flag on the Kinburn Spit after powerful fire damage forced Russian occupiers to retreat from their positions and evacuate surviving personnel. (Source: @UkraineNow/82170)

Commander Yuriy Harkavyi of the Skelia regiment was suspended pending a SBI investigation following a Babel publication about torture and deaths of mobilized soldiers in the unit. (Source: @UkraineNow/82178)

sections.executive_summary: Russia launched a large-scale overnight attack on Ukraine using an Iskander-M ballistic missile and 90 drones, striking civilian and energy infrastructure across multiple regions. Ukrainian forces retaliated by striking the Poltava oil base in Krasnodar Krai for the second time in a month, hitting at least three fuel tanks, and raised the national flag on the Kinburn Spit after forcing Russian forces to retreat. The EU disbursed €3.2 billion to Ukraine as the first tranche of a €90 billion reconstruction loan.

points: Ukrainian Defense Forces raised the blue-yellow flag on the Kinburn Spit after powerful fire damage forced Russian occupiers to retreat from their positions and evacuate surviving personnel, abandoning their defensive lines. (Source: @UkraineNow/82170)

Ukraine received €3.2 billion from the EU as the first macro-financial aid tranche within a €90 billion loan framework, with additional funds announced for drone production, announced by European Commission President Ursula von der Leyen at the Ukraine reconstruction conference in Gdansk. (Source: @UkraineNow/82171)

Commander Yuriy Harkavyi of the Skelia assault regiment was suspended from duties during an SBI investigation following a Babel publication alleging torture and deaths of mobilized soldiers in the unit; an acting commander has not yet been appointed. (Source: @UkraineNow/82178)

Ukrenergo CEO Vitaliy Zaychenko warned that if Russia resumes massive strikes on Ukraine's energy sector, power outage schedules will return, though the situation is significantly better now than a year ago. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31162)

Energy Minister Shmygal warned at the Gdansk reconstruction conference that the coming winter will be difficult and preparations must be made regardless of whether hostilities continue. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31169)

President Zelensky stated that Belarus, under Russian influence, is completing construction of roads and ammunition storage bases along the border with Ukraine, and that Ukraine has transmitted necessary signals regarding this activity and cooperation with Russia. (Source: @UkraineNow/82177)
Former Infrastructure Minister Pivovarsky stated that the Russian Armed Forces destroyed more than 150 gas stations in Ukraine in two months. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31168)

Russia launched an Iskander-M ballistic missile and 90 drones against Ukraine overnight, targeting gas stations in Zaporizhzhia and Sumy regions, injuring civilians including two gas station workers and five people in Bohodukhiv, Kharkiv region. (Source: @UkraineNow/82162)
Russia attacked energy infrastructure in Crimea overnight, leaving parts of the peninsula without electricity. (Source: @UkraineNow/82160)
Rosaviatsiya prohibited all flights from Russian airports to Iranian airports from June 14 to 24, between 23:00 and 11:00 MSK. (Source: @tass_agency/382417)

The Russian Embassy in Moldova protested after the detention of diplomatic couriers, with the Russian Foreign Ministry calling Moldovan law enforcement actions an unlawful demand to inspect diplomatic correspondence. (Source: @tass_agency/382419)

Senator Artem Sheikin told TASS that Apple's removal of VK from the App Store is an act of arbitrariness against Russian users, while the Russian Ministry of Digital Development called it a politically motivated decision and an act of unfair competition. (Source: @tass_agency/382395)

Kremlin spokesperson Dmitry Peskov urged Russians to switch to Android following Apple's removal of VK Music, VK Messenger, VK Video, VK Meetings, VK Calls, VK Admin, VK Player, VK Mail, Mail.ru Mail, Mail.ru Cloud, Mail.ru Zen, Odnoklassniki, Skillbox, Yula, and Marusya from the App Store. (Source: @UkraineNow/82173)

Five people were injured in Bohodukhiv, Kharkiv region following a Russian strike that damaged a building facade and power line, and set a garage and three vehicles on fire. (Source: @UkraineNow/82162)

Multiple Shahed-type and jet-powered BPLA were tracked across Kharkiv region including near Staryi Saltiv, Bohodukhiv district, heading southwest, with a threat of Shahed-136 launches from the Primorsko-Ahtarsk area with approximately five groups detected. (Source: @kpszsu/66375)
BPLA activity was reported near Novodonetsk in Donetsk region heading northwest, and reconnaissance BPLA was noted near Berezna in Chernihiv region. (Source: @kpszsu/66338)
Sumy region came under MLRS attack, with BPLA also tracked heading toward Shostka from the east and jet-powered BPLA in eastern Poltavshchyna heading northeast. (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68049)

BPLA were tracked on the northern Chernihiv region heading westward toward Lyubech and Slavutych, with one BPLA between Kulykivka and Nizhyn, and another heading south toward Korukivka. (Source: @eRadarrua/98150)
Jet-powered BPLA Geran-4/5 launches were reported from Oryol, with a jet BPLA also tracked toward Poltava from the north. (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68044)
Two BPLA were reported on Dnipropetrovshchyna from Zaporizhzhia heading toward Solone, and Sumy region faced BPLA from the north. (Source: @eRadarrua/98119)

Ukrainian Defense Forces raised the blue-yellow flag on the Kinburn Spit after powerful fire damage forced Russian occupiers to retreat from their positions and evacuate surviving personnel, abandoning their defensive lines. (Source: @UkraineNow/82170)
BPLA were tracked toward Zaporizhzhia from the south, toward Malokaterynivka and Novomykolaivka in Zaporizhzhia region, and reconnaissance BPLA was noted near Berezna. (Source: @kpszsu/66342)
BPLA were tracked toward Hlyny/Krasnokutsk in Kharkiv region heading southwest, and toward Novomykolaivka in Zaporizhzhia region heading northwest. (Source: @eRadarrua/98132)
BPLA threat was reported toward Odesa region from the Black Sea near Hryhorivka. (Source: @kpszsu/66339)

Russia launched an Iskander-M ballistic missile and 90 drones against Ukraine overnight, with the Iskander striking Kyiv's Darnytskyi district causing a warehouse fire and debris falling on open territory. (Source: @UkraineNow/82162)
All missiles that flew toward Kyiv were reportedly shot down according to available information. (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68047)
Among destroyed targets were Zircon missiles launched from Kursk region. (Source: @eRadarrua/98140)
Jet-powered BPLA Geran-4/5 launches were reported from Oryol, and approximately five groups of Shahed-136 were detected launching from the Primorsko-Ahtarsk area. (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68044)

key_developments: Russia launched a combined Iskander-M and 90-drone overnight attack on Ukraine, striking gas stations in Zaporizhzhia and Sumy regions and injuring multiple civilians including five in Bohodukhiv, Kharkiv region. (Source: @UkraineNow/82162)

Ukrainian forces struck the Poltava oil base in Krasnodar Krai for the second time in a month, hitting at least three fuel tanks that supply Kuban and the Republic of Adygea. (Source: @UkraineNow/82160)

Ukrainian Defense Forces raised the blue-yellow flag on the Kinburn Spit after fire damage forced Russian occupiers to retreat and evacuate, abandoning their defensive lines. (Source: @UkraineNow/82170)

The EU disbursed €3.2 billion to Ukraine as the first tranche of a €90 billion loan at the Gdansk reconstruction conference, with additional drone production funding announced. (Source: @UkraineNow/82171)
US Secretary of State Rubio confirmed no final agreement was reached between Putin and Trump at the Anchorage meeting, with only a proposal made on Ukraine settlement. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31160)

France detained the sanctioned shadow fleet tanker Deliver transiting near Sicily in the Mediterranean Sea, with President Macron confirming the vessel violated maritime law. (Source: @UkraineNow/82172)

Commander Yuriy Harkavyi of the Skelia assault regiment was suspended amid an SBI investigation into alleged torture and deaths of mobilized soldiers reported by Babel media. (Source: @UkraineNow/82178)
sections.threat_assessment: The operational environment is characterized by sustained Russian long-range strikes on Ukrainian civilian and energy infrastructure, with an Iskander-M and 90-drone salvo indicating continued capacity for complex combined attacks. Ukrainian forces demonstrated the ability to project strikes into Russian territory, hitting the Poltava oil base in Krasnodar Krai for a second time, while achieving a localized tactical success on the Kinburn Spit. The detection of approximately five Shahed-136 launch groups from Primorsko-Ahtarsk and Geran-4/5 launches from Oryol indicates Russia maintains substantial drone strike capacity across multiple axes.
sections.regional_response: The EU's disbursement of €3.2 billion as the first tranche of a €90 billion reconstruction loan signals sustained Western financial commitment to Ukraine's resilience. France's detention of the shadow fleet tanker Deliver near Sicily represents an escalation in maritime enforcement against Russia's sanctioned shipping operations. The US position remains uncertain following Rubio's confirmation that no agreement was reached at the Anchorage summit, with Deputy Secretary of State Jeremy Levin noting that the US endorses Zelensky's proposals while Putin disagrees, placing Trump in a difficult position.
sections.intelligence_notes: The SBI investigation into the Skelia regiment following Babel's report on torture and deaths of mobilized soldiers represents a significant internal accountability matter that could affect unit morale and operational effectiveness. Apple's removal of Russian applications including VK and Odnoklassniki from the App Store has prompted Russian officials to frame this as politically motivated, with Peskov urging citizens to switch to Android devices. Belarus's construction of roads and ammunition storage bases along the Ukrainian border under Russian influence, as noted by Zelensky, suggests continued Belarusian complicity in supporting Russian operational logistics.
